description: Refactor completo para eliminar classes de bugs recorrentes: unificar sign convention em money.py, inverter event ledger para WAL pattern, mover filtros para SQL, unificar refund handling, e adicionar testes de invariantes financeiras.

userStories.title: Fix integration test data paths
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the 3 integration test files to find their test data correctly, so that 119 test errors are resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: In testes/integration/test_dre_reconciliation.py lines 32-33: change Path(__file__).parent / 'data' to Path(__file__).parent.parent / 'data' for both CACHE_PATH and EXTRATO_PATH
In testes/integration/test_dre_reconciliation_fev2026.py lines 32-33: same change for CACHE_PATH and EXTRATO_PATH
In testes/integration/test_event_ledger_backfill.py lines 40-41: same change for CACHE_JAN and CACHE_FEV
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/integration/ -q --no-header 2>&1 | tail -5 — no FileNotFoundError
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Test data lives at testes/data/ but tests were moved to testes/integration/ without updating paths. This is a 3-line fix across 3 files.

userStories.title: Fix time-dependent test failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that depend on 'today' to use a mocked date, so they don't break every month.
acceptanceCriteria: Find all test files that call validate_extrato_coverage or depend on datetime.now() for assertions
Add monkeypatch to freeze the date to a fixed value within the CSV date range in those test fixtures
The key function is in app/services/extrato_ingester.py — it computes 'yesterday' via datetime.now(BRT). Mock this in tests
All 9 previously failing tests in testes/unit/test_extrato_multi_upload.py and testes/unit/test_extrato_classification.py now pass
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/unit/test_extrato_multi_upload.py testes/unit/test_extrato_classification.py -q — 0 failures
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: These tests create CSVs ending in past months but validate_extrato_coverage requires coverage through 'yesterday'. Freezing the date to within the CSV range fixes this. Use monkeypatch on datetime in the extrato_ingester module.

userStories.title: Create unified money.py sign convention module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single authoritative function for converting financial direction+amount to signed amount, so sign bugs can never diverge across modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/services/money.py with function: signed_amount(direction: str, amount: float) -> float
Supported directions: 'income' -> abs(amount), 'expense' -> -abs(amount), 'transfer_in' -> abs(amount), 'transfer_out' -> -abs(amount), 'deposit' -> abs(amount)
Any unknown direction raises ValueError with descriptive message
Create testes/unit/test_money.py with parametrized tests covering all directions
Test that signed_amount('income', -50.0) returns 50.0 (always abs for income)
Test that signed_amount('expense', 50.0) returns -50.0 (always negative for expense)
Test that signed_amount('unknown', 10) raises ValueError
Test edge cases: amount=0.0 for all directions returns 0.0
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/unit/test_money.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: This module replaces 6 independent _signed_amount functions scattered across the codebase. Keep it simple — one function, no classes, no dataclasses. The callers will be updated in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: Update processor.py to use money.signed_amount
userStories.description: As a developer, I want processor.py to use the unified money.signed_amount function instead of inline sign decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add import: from app.services.money import signed_amount at top of app/services/processor.py
In _process_approved(): replace signed_amount=amount with signed_amount=signed_amount('income', amount) for sale_approved
Replace signed_amount=-mp_fee with signed_amount=signed_amount('expense', mp_fee) for fee_charged
Replace signed_amount=-shipping with signed_amount=signed_amount('expense', shipping) for shipping_charged
Replace signed_amount=subsidy with signed_amount=signed_amount('income', subsidy) for subsidy_credited
In _process_refunded(): replace signed_amount=-refund with signed_amount=signed_amount('expense', refund) for refund_created and partial_refund
Replace signed_amount=refunded_fee with signed_amount=signed_amount('income', refunded_fee) for refund_fee
Replace signed_amount=refunded_shipping with signed_amount=signed_amount('income', refunded_shipping) for refund_shipping
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/unit/test_processor_unit.py testes/integration/test_processor_integration.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Mechanical replacement. The resulting signed values must be identical to before. Do NOT change the event ledger record_event call order yet (that is US-008). Note: the keyword argument name 'signed_amount' clashes with the imported function name. Rename the import to e.g. 'from app.services.money import signed_amount as compute_sign' or use money.signed_amount with a module import.

userStories.title: Update expense_classifier.py to use money.signed_amount
userStories.description: As a developer, I want expense_classifier.py to use the unified sign function, eliminating _expense_signed_amount.
acceptanceCriteria: Add import: from app.services.money import signed_amount (or module import) at top of app/services/expense_classifier.py
Remove the _expense_signed_amount() function (around line 263)
Keep the _is_incoming_transfer() helper — move its call to BEFORE the signed_amount call so callers determine direction first
Update all callers: if direction='income' use 'income', if direction='transfer' and _is_incoming_transfer()=True use 'transfer_in', else use 'expense'
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/unit/test_expense_classifier*.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes with no new failures
userStories.notes: The _is_incoming_transfer() function checks collector_id/payer_id to determine if a transfer is incoming. This logic must be preserved — just move it to determine the direction string before calling the unified signed_amount().

userStories.title: Update extrato_ingester.py to use money.signed_amount
userStories.description: As a developer, I want extrato_ingester.py to use the unified sign function, eliminating _extrato_signed_amount.
acceptanceCriteria: Add import from app.services.money at top of app/services/extrato_ingester.py
Remove the _extrato_signed_amount() function (around line 564)
Update all callers to use the unified signed_amount() with appropriate direction mapping
Map: direction='income' -> 'income', else -> 'expense'
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/unit/test_extrato*.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes with no new failures
userStories.notes: extrato_ingester.py is 1478 lines but _extrato_signed_amount is a simple function. The replacement is mechanical — find all calls and replace.

userStories.title: Update remaining 3 files to use money.signed_amount
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the last 3 files with local _signed_amount copies to use the unified function.
acceptanceCriteria: Update app/services/financial_closing.py: remove _signed_amount() (~line 51), replace calls. Map deposit/deposito_avulso expense_type to 'deposit' direction
Update app/services/release_report_sync.py: remove _release_signed_amount() (~line 161), replace calls
Update app/routers/expenses/_deps.py: remove _signed_amount() (~line 91), replace calls
Grep entire codebase for '_signed_amount' or '_expense_signed_amount' or '_extrato_signed_amount' or '_release_signed_amount' — zero results (only money.signed_amount should exist)
Run: python3 -m pytest -q — no new failures
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: These are the last 3 copies. After this story, grep for 'def _.*signed_amount' should return 0 results. The financial_closing.py version has special logic for deposit/deposito_avulso — map those to the 'deposit' direction in money.signed_amount.

userStories.title: Reorder processor.py to write-ahead log pattern
userStories.description: As a system, I want to record the event in the ledger BEFORE enqueuing the CA job, so the ledger is the real source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/services/processor.py _process_approved(): for each of the 4 event+enqueue pairs (sale_approved, fee_charged, shipping_charged, subsidy_credited), move record_event() BEFORE the enqueue_*() call
In _process_refunded(): for each of the 3 event+enqueue pairs (refund_created, refund_fee, refund_shipping), move record_event() BEFORE enqueue_*()
In partial refund path: move record_event('partial_refund') BEFORE enqueue_partial_refund()
If record_event() raises EventRecordError, the corresponding enqueue must NOT execute — let the exception propagate or return early
If enqueue_*() fails after event is recorded, log a warning with payment_id and event_type for manual retry
Add test in test_processor_integration.py: mock record_event to raise EventRecordError, assert enqueue_* was NOT called
Add test: mock enqueue_* to raise Exception, assert record_event WAS called successfully
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/integration/test_processor_integration.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Currently all 8 pairs do: enqueue first, then record event. If record_event fails, CA gets a duplicate on next sync. The fix swaps the order. The EventRecordError catch blocks after each enqueue need to move — now the try/except wraps the enqueue, not the record_event.

userStories.title: Move pagination filters to SQL in event_ledger.py
userStories.description: As a system, I want event_type filters applied in the database query, not post-fetch in Python, so pagination never silently drops data.
acceptanceCriteria: In get_dre_summary() (event_ledger.py ~line 426): add filter to exclude cash_* and expense_* event_types in the Supabase query builder BEFORE .execute()
Remove the Python list comprehension that filters rows post-fetch (~lines 451-454)
In get_payment_statuses() (~line 386): same SQL filter addition and Python filter removal (~lines 410-412)
If Supabase doesnt support NOT LIKE, use .filter('event_type', 'not.like', 'cash_%') or .not_.like('event_type', 'cash_%')
The pagination break condition (len(rows) < page_limit) now operates on pre-filtered rows from the DB
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/unit/test_event_ledger*.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Current post-fetch filtering causes pagination to exit early when most rows in a page are cash/expense events. This silently drops remaining financial events. Supabase Python SDK supports .not_.like() or .filter('col', 'not.like', 'pattern%').

userStories.title: Unify partial and full refund fee/shipping handling
userStories.description: As a system, I want partial refunds to correctly create fee and shipping reversal events when ML actually refunded those charges.
acceptanceCriteria: In processor.py _process_refunded(): replace 'if refunded_fee > 0 and estorno_receita >= amount' (~line 574) with 'if refunded_fee > 0'
Same for shipping: replace 'if refunded_shipping > 0 and estorno_receita >= amount' (~line 593) with 'if refunded_shipping > 0'
The refunded_fee and refunded_shipping values already come from charges_details[].amounts.refunded which is the correct source
Add integration test: partial refund where ML refunded fees — assert refund_fee event IS created
Add integration test: partial refund where ML did NOT refund fees (refunded_fee=0) — assert refund_fee event is NOT created
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/integration/test_processor_integration.py testes/unit/test_processor_unit.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Currently partial refunds (estorno_receita < amount) NEVER emit refund_fee or refund_shipping events, even when ML actually refunded the fees. The condition should only check whether charges_details reports a refund, not whether its a full refund.

userStories.title: Add zero-sum ledger invariant tests
userStories.description: As a financial system, I want tests that verify the algebraic sum of events per payment lifecycle equals expected net.
acceptanceCriteria: Create testes/unit/test_ledger_invariants.py with parametrized test scenarios
Scenario 1 - Approved: sale_approved(+1000) + fee_charged(-150) + shipping_charged(-50) = 800
Scenario 2 - Full refund: all events sum to 0
Scenario 3 - Partial refund by amount: sale_approved(+1000) + fee_charged(-150) + partial_refund(-300) = 550
Scenario 4 - Chargeback reimbursed: sale_approved(+1000) + fee_charged(-150) + charged_back(-1000) + reimbursed(+1000) = 850
Each scenario validates sum(signed_amounts) matches expected_balance with tolerance 0.01
All signed_amount values pass event_ledger.validate_event() for their event_type (import and call it)
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/unit/test_ledger_invariants.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Pure unit tests, no DB needed. They validate that EVENT_TYPES sign conventions produce balanced books. Import validate_event from app.services.event_ledger to verify each amount is valid for its event_type.

userStories.title: Add CA payload value assertions to integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests to verify the actual amounts inside CA payloads, not just call counts.
acceptanceCriteria: In testes/integration/test_processor_integration.py: for every assert_called_once() on enqueue_*, add assertions on call_args payload values
For enqueue_receita: assert the valor in payload equals payment amount (positive float)
For enqueue_comissao: assert valor equals mp_fee (positive float)
For enqueue_frete: assert valor equals shipping cost (positive float)
For enqueue_estorno: assert valor equals refund amount (positive float)
For enqueue_estorno_taxa: assert valor equals refunded_fee (positive float)
Assert data_competencia matches expected BRT date for at least one test case
Run: python3 -m pytest testes/integration/test_processor_integration.py -v — all pass
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Access payload via mock.call_args. Pattern: mock_cq.enqueue_receita.call_args — check positional args or kwargs for the payload dict containing 'valor'. The exact payload structure depends on how enqueue_receita is called in processor.py — read the code to determine arg positions.
